regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 18, 2026: Current conditions at San Juan show 74°F with ESE winds at 6 knots and partly cloudy skies; rain chances remain low at 8 percent. Expect a gradual increase in winds to 14 knots from the ESE later today with overcast skies and a 10 percent chance of isolated showers. Similar conditions will persist through St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos with SE to southerly winds 10-14 knots and minimal rain risk. Mariners should note smooth seas and good visibility; fishing captains can plan for stable conditions but remain alert for brief overcast periods. Passengers should carry light rain gear for occasional cloud cover, while yacht operators can enjoy favorable anchorage conditions with moderate easterly trade winds.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean conditions for March 18, 2026, show moderate northerly winds across key ports including Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth. Current winds at Cozumel are NNE at 14 knots with scattered rain showers but no significant precipitation expected today or over the next three days. Mariners should expect seas building slightly with steady 15-17 knot winds from the north to NNE, producing manageable choppy conditions ideal for day operations; no Small Craft Advisories are in effect. Fishing and yacht captains are advised to secure light gear to prevent water intrusion during brief showers, especially near Cozumel and Roatan anchorages, while cruise passengers should carry light rain protection but anticipate mostly dry conditions with partial sun tomorrow.
regions.bahamas: Today in the Bahamas region, expect overcast skies with light east-northeast winds at 4 knots and a 69 percent chance of rain, mainly scattered showers near Nassau. Through the next three days, winds will shift northward, increasing to 20 knots by day three with diminishing drizzle and improving conditions, especially around Freeport, Bimini, and Turks & Caicos. Mariners should anticipate choppy seas but no Small Craft Advisories; caution is advised when operating smaller vessels or during fishing near exposed anchorages. Cruise passengers should keep rain gear handy for intermittent showers, and yacht captains should monitor wind shifts and maintain secure moorings in the Berry Islands and Turks & Caicos.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather on March 18, 2026 will be stable with east winds 14 to 20 knots and mostly partly cloudy skies. Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena can expect daytime temperatures near 81-82°F with minimal rain chances under 5 percent. Mariners should note a Small Craft Advisory level breeze near Aruba, with seas likely moderate; secure loose gear and maintain vigilant lookouts for gusts. Fishing captains will find good conditions for offshore trips, while yacht operators should monitor local sea state, especially around shallower anchorages in Bonaire where east winds may increase chop.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today are mild with temperatures near 78°F, light NNE winds at 5 knots, and partly cloudy skies. Rain chances are low at 23% but will increase sharply starting tomorrow with thunderstorms expected through March 20, accompanied by N to NNE winds strengthening to 13-17 knots. Mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and locally rough seas near Grand Cayman, Belize City, and coastal waters off Roatan and Cozumel. Cruise passengers are advised to secure loose items on deck; fishing and yacht captains should monitor lightning risk and consider sheltered anchorages, especially in exposed areas like Costa Maya, until storm activity subsides.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles update for March 18, 2026: Barbados reports steady ENE winds at 19 knots with partly cloudy skies and a low rain chance near 18 percent. Expect similar conditions region-wide with moderate trade winds easing slightly to 15-17 knots by Day 3. Seas remain moderate; no Small Craft Advisories or warnings are in effect. Mariners should maintain routine vigilance—yacht captains should ensure lines and fenders are secure in exposed anchorages such as St. Lucia’s Rodney Bay, where easterly trades prevail. Cruise passengers can anticipate comfortable conditions for shore excursions, but carry light rain gear for brief passing showers.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and nearby ports are experiencing light fog this morning under calm SSE winds at 5 knots and mild 62°F temps. Expect fog to persist through Day 2 with light SW to WSW winds 7-9 knots and highs in the upper 70s to low 80s, keeping visibility reduced especially near Catalina Island and Marina del Rey. Day 3 brings clearing skies with a light increase to 88°F and steady SW winds around 8 knots; no precipitation expected during the period. Mariners should maintain vigilance for fog-related visibility restrictions when navigating into Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach harbors; fishing captains should monitor local conditions closely for safe anchorage, especially in Catalina’s coves where fog may delay safe departures.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada and surrounding waters will remain foggy through March 19 with light WSW winds 2-6 knots and no precipitation expected. Mariners should exercise caution entering or departing Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos due to reduced visibility in fog conditions; use radar and AIS for situational awareness. On March 20, expect a sharp rise in temperature to 90°F with clear skies and a light southwest breeze around 7 knots, improving visibility and sea conditions for fishing and yachting. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities accordingly, as fog delays may affect morning port operations the next two days.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will experience stable conditions today with temperatures near 70 to 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Northwest winds will remain steady at 12 to 15 knots, producing moderate seas ideal for small craft and fishing operations without Small Craft Advisories in effect. Skies will be partly cloudy with zero precipitation expected, providing good visibility and comfortable conditions for day excursions and anchorage at all main ports. Vessel operators should maintain routine watch on wind shifts but can expect smooth navigation and safe conditions for both passenger activities and commercial fishing through March 20.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and nearby ports Topolobampo and Altata will experience calm and stable conditions today with light ESE winds at 1 knot, clear skies, and no precipitation expected. Winds will shift to WNW 8-9 knots over the next two days with partly cloudy skies, then back to light SW winds and clear conditions by day three. Mariners can expect smooth seas and excellent visibility, making it ideal for fishing and recreational boating. Anchorage in all local ports remains safe with minimal wind-driven surge or wave action; routine docking and tender operations can proceed without weather concerns.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is experiencing mild conditions with light NNE winds at 1 knot and partly cloudy skies, temperature steady near 76°F. Over the next three days, expect west to west-southwest winds increasing to 6-7 knots with mostly clear to partly cloudy skies and zero precipitation chances. Mariners should find calm seas ideal for navigation and anchoring at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, with no marine wind warnings in effect. Fishing captains and yacht operators can plan for stable conditions but remain vigilant for any sudden changes in local sea breeze patterns during afternoon hours.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 18, 2026: Barcelona reports clear skies, light northwest winds at 2 knots, and a cool 49°F with no rain expected today. Expect increasing easterly winds up to 14 knots and patchy fog tonight with a 30% chance of light rain, especially near coastal ports like Valencia and Palma de Mallorca. By March 19, moderate ENE winds near 12 knots will bring scattered showers but minimal rainfall accumulation. Mariners should maintain vigilance for reduced visibility in fog-prone anchorages such as Ibiza and Gibraltar overnight. Cruise and fishing vessels should prepare for calm, clear conditions today but monitor forecast updates for possible delays or navigational adjustments due to fog and light rain over the next 48 hours.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 18, 2026: Naples currently reports 49°F with light northeast winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies. Expect a rise in winds from the north-northeast to 6-10 knots over the next two days with increasing cloud cover and a 20-48% chance of rain showers, particularly on day one. Mariners should prepare for wet decks and reduced visibility near Naples, Civitavecchia, and Genoa; moderate northerly winds will produce calm to moderate seas, no small craft advisories expected. Cruise passengers should carry rain gear for day one and anticipate improving conditions by day three. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor localized showers near coastal anchorage points in Sardinia and Corsica and plan for light winds and overcast skies by day three.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 18, 2026:

Athens (Piraeus) currently steady at 54°F with light NE winds at 2 knots and overcast skies; rain chances remain low today near 10%. Expect increasing rain chances over the next three days with NNE winds strengthening to 7-9 knots and intermittent showers forecast through March 20. Yacht and fishing captains should prepare for reduced visibility and slick decks during rainy periods, especially near Athens, Rhodes, and Izmir, where overcast and showers will be more persistent. Passengers in Santorini, Mykonos, and Cyprus should carry rain gear and plan limited outdoor activities; harbor conditions remain calm with no advisories for small craft at this time.
